subtasks.description: Call add_task_evidence to record strengths/weaknesses summary
completion_evidence: Tool fetch_ticker_repository_data returned substantial text output
observations:
Tool 'fetch_ticker_repository_data' returned: count: 111
items:
- date: '2025-03-31'
  dcf: 2.0
  de: 4.0
  overallScore: 3.0
  pb: 1.0
  pe: 1.0
  rating: B
  roa: 5.0
  roe: 4.0
- date: '2025-04-01'
  dcf: 2.0
  de: 4.0
  overallScore: 3.0
  pb

completion_evidence:
Synthesis from baseline analytics:
Strengths:
- Strong risk-adjusted profile overall: Sharpe 1.9771, Sortino 3.0279, MDD -20.4%, ann return 69.3% with beta 1.05.
- Low average pairwise correlation (0.269), aided by mix of tech, EM single-country ETFs (EIS, GREK), and commodity ETFs (SLVR, SGDM).
- Positive quality tilt (net +0.226) supports resilience; strong performers include PLTR (Sharpe 2.665), APP (2.21), CRDO (2.21), SLVR (2.459), SGDM (1.854), EIS (1.527), GREK (1.609), NRG (1.206), JPM (1.368).
- Utilities and commodities provide ballast and stagflation upside; portfolio gains in stagflation scenario (+3.85%).

Weaknesses / Risks:
- Clustered correlations: Industrial REITs highly redundant (FR–TRNO corr 0.902; STAG–TRNO 0.858); Precious metals ETFs highly redundant (SGDM–SLVR 0.829); Semis cluster tightly (AVGO–NVDA 0.757; ARM/MTSI/CRDO/MU all >0.70 vs NVDA).
- Underperformers by Sharpe: IIPR (-0.736), EQIX (0.186), STAG (0.073), FR (0.303), TRNO (0.282), CMS (0.516), ARM (0.627), MU (0.678), MTSI (0.523), FITB (0.561), EMQQ (0.93).
- Stress vulnerabilities: Hypothetical credit crunch -20.7% P&L; tech/semi heavy drawdowns (CRDO -47.9%, OKLO -47.2%, MU -43.3%, PLTR -38.6%).
- Factor tilts: Negative momentum (-0.029), negative value (-0.08), negative low-vol (-0.128) raise drawdown risk; high concentration in a few names (top-10 62% gross; EQIX 8.1%, NVDA 6.5%).
- Redundant low-vol sleeves: VCSH and IGSB corr 0.864; both not needed.

Implications:
- Trim/remove industrial REIT complex and underperforming REITs; keep best-in-class or replace with uncorrelated exposures.
- Consolidate precious metals to single sleeve (favor SLVR over SGDM on Sharpe).
- Deconcentrate semis to 1–2 anchors (NVDA +/- AVGO); remove small, highly correlated semis (ARM, CRDO, MTSI, MU) to cut beta/correlation.
- Simplify banks to JPM (best Sharpe), drop small/regional/EM duplicates if needed.
- Keep utilities but reduce duplication in multi-utilities (CMS/AEE/WEC highly correlated); retain the strongest.
- Keep PLTR/APP as growth-quality leaders; reassess micro-caps/speculative (OKLO, DGNX, FUFU, EXOD) given stress risk and momentum tilt.

observations: Tool 'episodic_remember' returned: {'timestamp': '2025-09-25T21:54:02.460126', 'title': 'Final portfolio stored & rationale', 'event': 'final_portfolio_memory', 'outcome': 'Final 13-position portfolio saved (gross=0.95, net=0.95): NVDA

subtasks.description: Optionally Call add_task_evidence with final snapshots
observations: Tool 'add_task_evidence' returned: True

completion_evidence:
Per-position one-sentence theses (tool-derived):
- NVDA: AI semi anchor with Sharpe 1.17, max DD -37.2%, and negative corr with WEC (-0.166) to diversify tech cyclicality.
- AVGO: High-quality semi with Sharpe 1.48, alpha 0.50, strong up-capture; complements NVDA.
- PLTR: Momentum/quality leader (Sharpe 2.66, Sortino 4.72) driving alpha with positive growth tilt.
- APP: Growth name with strong risk-adjusted returns (Sharpe 2.21) and robust up-capture; sized to control beta.
- NRG: Utilities alpha (Sharpe 1.21, max DD -26.2%) and positive stagflation payoff.
- WEC: Defensive utility (Sharpe 1.19, ann vol 0.168) with negative corr to NVDA (-0.166), improving resilience.
- JPM: Best-in-class bank (Sharpe 1.37, steady up-capture) offering balanced financials exposure.
- BSX: Low-beta medtech ballast (Sharpe 0.85, ulcer 0.040) with consistent growth.
- SLVR: High-conviction commodity sleeve (Sharpe 2.46, Calmar 7.67) with low equity correlation and inflation hedge.
- EIS: EM single-country ETF with Sharpe 1.53 and diversification benefits vs U.S. tech.
- GREK: EM Greece ETF with Sharpe 1.61 and strong 6M momentum (0.494) for differentiated cyclicality.
- VCSH: Low-vol IG credit ballast (ann vol 0.025, max DD -1.54%) with low equity correlations.
- NLR: Thematic uranium exposure (Sharpe 1.14) adding commodity-linked diversification.

Final portfolio (allocations sum=0.95): {"NVDA":0.085,"AVGO":0.075,"PLTR":0.075,"APP":0.06,"NRG":0.07,"WEC":0.075,"JPM":0.085,"BSX":0.075,"SLVR":0.09,"EIS":0.08,"GREK":0.07,"VCSH":0.07,"NLR":0.04}. Final analytics: Beta=1.063, Sharpe=2.339, Sortino=3.498, AnnReturn=0.843, AnnVol=0.259, MaxDD=-0.208, Avg pairwise corr ≈0.30–0.32; Factor tilts: growth +0.034, momentum +0.086, quality +0.121, value -0.055, low-vol -0.028; Stress worst case: credit_crunch ≈ -22.0%, stagflation +4.8%.

execution_history.evidence: Improvement deltas vs baseline and v1: Baseline beta 1.051 → Final 1.063 (≈flat vs baseline, ↓ vs v1=1.156); Sharpe 1.977 → 2.339 (↑18.3%); Sortino 3.028 → 3.498 (↑15.5%); MaxDD -20.4% → -20.8% (≈flat); AnnReturn 0.693 → 0.843 (↑21.6%); AnnVol 0.264 → 0.259 (↓). Average correlation modestly higher vs baseline (0.27→~0.30–0.32) but diversified by negative/low-corr sleeves (WEC, VCSH, SLVR). Stress worst case credit crunch: -20.7% → -22.0% (slightly worse), stagflation +3.85% → +4.8% (better). Factor tilts improved from negative momentum/growth to positive across momentum (+0.0856), growth (+0.0343), quality (+0.1209).

observations: Drafted full change log: removed 22 tickers (ARM, CRDO, MTSI, MU, EQIX, IIPR, FR, STAG, TRNO, EMQQ, AEE, CMS, CEG, FITB, INTR, NU, DGNX, FUFU, EXOD, OKLO, IGSB, SGDM); retained and resized 13 tickers (NVDA, AVGO, PLTR, APP, NRG, WEC, JPM, BSX, SLVR, EIS, GREK, VCSH, NLR) with explicit old→new weights

execution_history.evidence:
Per-position one-sentence theses (tool-derived):
- NVDA: AI semi anchor with Sharpe 1.17, max DD -37.2%, and negative corr with WEC (-0.166) to diversify tech cyclicality.
- AVGO: High-quality semi with Sharpe 1.48, alpha 0.50, strong up-capture; complements NVDA.
- PLTR: Momentum/quality leader (Sharpe 2.66, Sortino 4.72) driving alpha with positive growth tilt.
- APP: Growth name with strong risk-adjusted returns (Sharpe 2.21) and robust up-capture; sized to control beta.
- NRG: Utilities alpha (Sharpe 1.21, max DD -26.2%) and positive stagflation payoff.
- WEC: Defensive utility (Sharpe 1.19, ann vol 0.168) with negative corr to NVDA (-0.166), improving resilience.
- JPM: Best-in-class bank (Sharpe 1.37, steady up-capture) offering balanced financials exposure.
- BSX: Low-beta medtech ballast (Sharpe 0.85, ulcer 0.040) with consistent growth.
- SLVR: High-conviction commodity sleeve (Sharpe 2.46, Calmar 7.67) with low equity correlation and inflation hedge.
- EIS: EM single-country ETF with Sharpe 1.53 and diversification benefits vs U.S. tech.
- GREK: EM Greece ETF with Sharpe 1.61 and strong 6M momentum (0.494) for differentiated cyclicality.
- VCSH: Low-vol IG credit ballast (ann vol 0.025, max DD -1.54%) with low equity correlations.
- NLR: Thematic uranium exposure (Sharpe 1.14) adding commodity-linked diversification.
